meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Finish SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first SaaS as a student.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of defined client constraints. By adopting a rigid client workflow, you transform open-ended coding into structured deliverables. Use Antigravity to define the scope and Claude Code to execute the build. This shift moves you from 'endless tinkering' to 'shippable product'.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is littered with the corpses of 'perfect' ideas. You start with high-octane motivation, building the login page, the database, and the UI, only to hit the wall of feature creep and complexity. The silence of an unfinished project is the loudest sound a developer hears.

But the gap between a student and a senior engineer isn't raw coding speed—it's the workflow. Professionals don't just 'code'; they manage client requirements, even when they are their own client. By treating your personal project like a high-stakes agency contract, you inject the necessary friction to force completion.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope with Antigravity
steps.description: Stop building features based on 'what if' scenarios. Use Antigravity to generate a strict, non-negotiable feature list that fits a 14-day sprint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior project manager. Create a 14-day MVP scope for a [Insert Project Idea] that focuses only on the core value proposition. Provide a list of 5 essential features and 3 'out-of-scope' items to avoid feature creep.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a project roadmap file based on the Antigravity output'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Don't write a single line of business logic until the schema is locked. A solid database is the backbone of project completion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for the MVP defined in step 1. Provide the SQL or Prisma schema and explain how it supports the core user journey.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate database migration files based on the schema provided by Antigravity'

steps.title: Systematic Component Build
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build components in isolation, ensuring each one meets the requirements defined in your initial scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a component dependency graph for the MVP. Which components are critical for the login and core user flow?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build the authentication component following the dependency graph'

steps.title: Implement Client Feedback Loops
steps.description: Even if you are the client, document your progress daily. This prevents the 'I forgot what I was building' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 'Client Feedback Log' template that I can use to evaluate my progress every 48 hours for this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a progress_log.md file and initialize it with today's status based on the codebase'

steps.title: Final QA and Deployment
steps.description: Shift from building to shipping. Use Claude Code to run automated tests and prepare the production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a checklist for production readiness, including environment variables, security audits, and deployment steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run all tests and provide a summary of production-ready status'

pro_tips: Treat your project like a billable contract; if a feature isn't in the initial scope, it doesn't exist.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code every 3 days to prevent technical debt from slowing you down.
Always build the 'Happy Path' first; edge cases are the primary cause of project abandonment.
Implement a 'Done-Done' definition: a feature is only finished when it is deployed to a staging URL.
Prioritize UI libraries over custom CSS; design-heavy tasks are the biggest time-sinks for students.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, deploy it to Vercel, share the link on Twitter/X, and start scoping your next, slightly more complex challenge.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every expert was once a student who refused to quit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
